special_pays_monthly.SF_SDAP: Varies by qualification — $300 to $1,000/mo for SF-qualified soldiers
special_pays_monthly.language_pay: $100 to $500/mo based on proficiency level and language criticality

tuition_assistance.note: Updated FY24-25: annual cap raised to $4,500, semester hours raised to 18. No service obligation for enlisted. Request BEFORE class start date — no exceptions. Cannot use TA and GI Bill for same course simultaneously.
tuition_assistance.strategy: Use TA while on active duty, save Post-9/11 GI Bill for post-EAS/retirement.

GI_Bill_Ch33.max_benefit: 100% in-state tuition + monthly housing allowance at E-5 w/dep BAH rate + $1,000/yr books
GI_Bill_Ch33.housing_allowance_note: Housing stipend is the most valuable part — only available when NOT on active duty. Wasted if used while serving.

BRS_retirement.applies_to: All soldiers who entered service after January 1, 2018
BRS_retirement.tsp_dod_match: DoD matches up to 5% of base pay after 2 years TIS
BRS_retirement.vesting: Immediate vesting on TSP contributions. DoD match vests at 2 years.

financial_rules.rule1: No car lots near post. Buy used with cash or USAA/PenFed credit union loan. Never dealership financing near a base.
financial_rules.rule2: Open Roth TSP Day 1. Contribute 5%+ for full BRS match (kicks in after 2 yrs TIS).
financial_rules.rule3: BAH strategy: find housing below BAH rate, invest the spread monthly.
financial_rules.rule4: VA loan: 0% down, no PMI. Buy at 2+ yr duty station. Rent at PCS. Multi-use.
financial_rules.rule5: Reenlistment bonuses (SRBs): invest immediately — TSP or Roth IRA.
financial_rules.rule6: Combat zone tax exclusion: all pay in designated zones is federal income tax-free. Max Roth IRA during deployment.
financial_rules.net_worth_target: Disciplined soldier on Ranger/SF track following all rules: $200,000+ net worth before 20-year retirement is realistic.
